prompt: We're a marketing agency that's been charging $5K/month retainer for social media management for the past 3 years. Our clients never push back on price and several have said we're a great deal. We've added AI content generation and analytics dashboards since we set the price. Should we raise our rates and how?

assertions.text: Response identifies multiple underpricing signals from the scenario (no pushback, 'great deal' feedback, years without change)
Response recommends a specific price increase range or new price point (not just 'raise your prices')
Response recommends HOW to implement the increase (grandfather, delayed, restructure, etc.)
Response connects the AI content generation and analytics dashboards to justifying the increase
Response addresses the importance of not setting-and-forgetting pricing (revisiting regularly)
Response addresses how to mitigate risk of client churn from the increase
